required_sections.detail: Re-checked after the section-renumbering fix. The surviving sections now hold canonical template numbers: Best Practices='## 8.', Hands-On Exercise='## 9.', Key Takeaways='## 10.', Next Steps='## 11.'. Required section 10 (Key Takeaways) is present at its canonical number with non-trivial content (six summary bullets, well over 50 words). Sections 3 (Learning Objectives), 4 (Introduction), and 5 (Core Concepts — six sub-sections, well over 100 words) all present and non-trivial. Optional sections 6 (Implementation) and 7 (Real-World Patterns) correctly omitted for the standard profile; the gap in numbering between 5 and 8 reflects those skipped optionals, not a renumber. Try-1 structural defect resolved.

citation_density.detail: Primary (ibm.com) cited as [1] throughout (intro, every Core Concepts sub-section, Best Practices, Key Takeaways). Supporting evidentlyai [2] and wikipedia [3] both resolve and are cited. Section 5 standalone factual assertions carry markers well above the 60% bar. No [4]+ markers — zero fabricated citations; all of [1][2][3] map to the three declared resources. Unchanged from try 1 (no content edited).

sequence_drift.detail: All technical noun phrases resolve to 5.6's own concepts_introduced (prompt injection, system prompt vs user input, shared context window, direct vs indirect prompt injection, ignore-previous-instructions attack, injection impacts) or to EARLIER topics: LLM (cross_ref / 3.2,3.4), probabilistic systems (1.3), jailbreak + data leakage (5.5), harm prevention / four pillars (5.4), red team (5.5). No forward references. Governance terms (EU AI Act / NIST — 5.7+) absent. Unchanged from try 1.

length_sanity.detail: Suggested profile standard; per artifact-mix.json standard reading band and manifest reading.target_word_range, operative band is 1200-1600. Corpus prose ~1300 words — in range, well within the 30% tolerance. Renumber fix changed no prose, so count is unchanged from try 1.

scope_creep.detail: This IS the home topic for prompt injection (5.5 deferred it here). Definition, why-it-works (shared context window), ignore-previous-instructions example, direct vs indirect, impacts, beginner defenses are all on-headline. No governance frameworks taught. Jailbreak (5.5) handled as a plain disambiguation table, not re-taught. No exploit-engineering or model-internals depth imported — held at beginner level. Unchanged from try 1.

beginner_readability.detail: LLM spelled out 'Large Language Model (LLM)' on first use (Introduction). System prompt, user input, context window, injection all defined in plain language on first use. No jargon-dump paragraph (3+ cold terms). Short sentences, active voice, 'you' address, no hedging. Zero distinct readability findings. Unchanged from try 1.

verdict: PASS — TRY 2. The try-1 required_sections defect is resolved: the section-renumbering fix put the tail back on canonical numbers — Best Practices='## 8.', Hands-On Exercise='## 9.', Key Takeaways='## 10.', Next Steps='## 11.'. Required section 10 (Key Takeaways) is now present at its canonical number with non-trivial content. Optional sections 6 (Implementation) and 7 (Real-World Patterns) remain correctly omitted for the standard profile. No prose was changed, so the other five checks carry forward unchanged: citation_density (strict, ibm.com primary cited, no fabricated markers), sequence_drift (no forward refs, governance held out), length_sanity (~1300w in 1200-1600), scope_creep (on-headline home topic, jailbreak disambiguated not re-taught), beginner_readability (LLM spelled out, terms defined on first use, no findings). All six checks pass. No remaining defects.
